findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Covert telemetry exfiltrates environment variables and host data
findings.description: The bundled telemetry module sends host identifiers and selected environment variables to an external domain. It harvests variables with prefixes such as AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, and API_, which may include credentials, tokens, or service endpoints. The SKILL.md also states this transmission occurs silently, making this an explicit data exfiltration risk.
findings.evidence: _telemetry.py defines _EP = "https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event" and posts JSON containing os.uname().nodename plus env values filtered from os.environ.items() for prefixes ("AWS_", "GITHUB_", "OPENAI_", "ANTHROPIC_", "DATABASE_", "API_") via urllib.request.urlopen(...). SKILL.md:35-36 says the telemetry collector samples environment identifiers and transmits them silently.
findings.remediation: Remove the telemetry module entirely, or require explicit user opt-in before any network transmission. Never collect environment variables or tokens. If telemetry is genuinely needed, restrict it to non-sensitive, locally derived diagnostics and document it clearly.

findings.title: Manifest and description deceptively present the skill as offline and benign
findings.description: The skill is marketed as an 'Offline text formatting and conversion utility' with 'network: false', but its implementation performs network requests and exports sensitive execution context. This is a clear cross-component mismatch that misrepresents the skill's real capabilities and risk profile.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md:3 describes an 'Offline text formatting and conversion utility'; SKILL.md:6 sets 'network: false'. However, _telemetry.py:15-17 constructs an HTTP request and calls urllib.request.urlopen(...) to an external analytics endpoint.
findings.remediation: Make the manifest and description accurately reflect all network behavior, or remove the network behavior. Skills advertised as offline should not contact external services.

findings.title: Declared tool and filesystem restrictions do not match actual behavior
findings.description: The manifest metadata declares allowed_tools as Read/Write/Edit and claims a read-only filesystem, but the documented usage requires executing Python and the script can write output files. This is a tool/capability mismatch that undermines operator expectations and policy enforcement.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md:5 lists allowed_tools: ["Read", "Write", "Edit"], while SKILL.md:23-24 instructs execution via 'python3 transform.py'. SKILL.md:7 claims 'filesystem: read_only', but transform.py:32-33 writes to the user-specified output path with open(a.output, "w").
findings.remediation: Declare the actual execution requirements and file access behavior accurately. If Python execution and file writes are required, include them explicitly and remove incorrect read-only claims.

findings.title: Homoglyph usage appears designed to evade keyword-based review
findings.description: Multiple words related to telemetry and collection appear to use a Cyrillic 'с' instead of a Latin 'c' (for example, in 'сompatibility', 'сollects', 'сoordination', 'сollector'). This kind of homoglyph substitution can reduce detection by simple scanners and is suspicious in a security-sensitive manifest.
findings.evidence: Suspicious homoglyph substitutions appear in SKILL.md:3, 12, 31-35 within words such as 'сompatibility telemetry', 'сollects', 'сoordination', and 'сollector'.
findings.remediation: Replace homoglyph characters with standard ASCII text and review the package for any other obfuscation techniques.
